data.title: Hybrid Mamba-Attention Neural Architecture for Channel Estimation
data.description: arXiv:2601.17108v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid Mamba-attention neural architecture to achieve improved channel estimation for orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) waveforms, particularly for configurations with a large number of subcarriers. By integrating a customized Mamba module, the proposed framework handles large-scale subcarrier channel estimation efficiently while capturing long-distance dependencies among these subcarriers effectively. Unlike the conventional Mamba structure, this paper implements a bidirectional selective scan to enable information propagation from both directions, because channel gains at different subcarriers are inherently non-causal. In addition, by integrating Mamba to reduce the reliance on quadratic-complexity self-attention, the proposed solution achieves lower space complexity than fully transformer architectures. Simulation results based on the 3GPP TS 36.101 channel demonstrate that compared to other baseline neural networks, the proposed method achieves superior channel estimation performance with fewer tunable parameters and exhibits good generalization across previously unseen channels.
data.author: Dianxin Luan, Chengsi Liang, Jie Huang, Zheng Lin, Kaitao Meng, John Thompson, Cheng-Xiang Wang
